commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d980b3f4ca6d7525726c10580701373c897c3d3e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a ContextEngineRuntimeSettings contract and passes runtimeSettings through context-engine bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, and maintenance paths across embedded, CLI, and Codex app-server runs.

PR surface: Source +267, Tests +111. Total +378 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence repro path: a strict context-engine plugin that rejects unknown lifecycle keys receives runtimeSettings, the current registry only strips sessionKey/prompt, and the runtime can quarantine the engine after the thrown error.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs contributor real-behavior proof plus maintainer-visible plugin API compatibility review, so it is not a safe automated repair lane.

target.title: feat(context-engine): pass runtime settings into lifecycle
target.body:
## Summary

Adds an OpenClaw-owned `ContextEngineRuntimeSettings` contract and passes it into context-engine lifecycle calls.

This is intentionally framed as a general OpenClaw context-engine programming contract, not a HyperMem-specific shim. Core now builds a typed runtime settings object from the active host/runtime/model context and supplies it to lifecycle hooks so context engines do not need to infer runtime facts from globals, logs, provider strings, or side channels.

What changed:

- Adds exported `ContextEngineRuntimeSettings` plus typed unavailable/unsupported runtime-settings errors.
- Adds `buildContextEngineRuntimeSettings()` in `src/context-engine/runtime-settings.ts`.
- Passes `runtimeSettings` into `bootstrap`, `assemble`, `afterTurn`, and `maintain` params.
- Wires runtime settings through the shared harness lifecycle, Codex app-server attempt path, embedded runner attempt path, generic CLI runner path, and deferred context-engine maintenance.
- Keeps lifecycle param additions optional for legacy context-engine compatibility.

## Rebase

Rebased onto the signed `v2026.5.31-beta.3` tag (`3fcd8e08fb`) and force-pushed the PR branch.

GitHub PR base is `release/2026.5.31`, because GitHub PRs target branches rather than tags. At rebase time that branch was 3 non-overlapping commits ahead of `v2026.5.31-beta.3` in UI/i18n and voice-call test files; this PR diff remains the same 9 runtime-settings files.

Current head: `d980b3f4ca6d`.

## Validation

Run after rebasing onto `v2026.5.31-beta.3`:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/context-engine/runtime-settings.test.ts src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts
git diff --check
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts src/agents/cli-runner.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/context-engine-maintenance.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.ts src/context-engine/runtime-settings.test.ts src/context-engine/runtime-settings.ts src/context-engine/types.ts
pnpm check:test-types
```

Observed result:

- Focused Vitest: 2 shards passed, 13 tests passed.
- `git diff --check`: passed.
- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`: already up to date.
- `oxfmt --check`: passed on all 9 touched files.
- `pnpm check:test-types`: passed.

## Notes

This is the narrow lifecycle spine. Follow-up work can extend public SDK docs/exports and richer doctor display after the active slot/compaction PRs settle, but the core runtime-settings contract is useful independently.
